commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=91deab9fbef87ddfc05dfd5bc75395581838e152)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex native conversation-binding dynamic-tool projection and dispatch, passes runtime config into the binding, and adds regression tests for tool specs, handler dispatch, channel context, and legacy sidecar preservation.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main starts the native bound thread without dynamicTools and returns a fixed unsupported response for item/tool/call. I did not run a live bound Codex conversation in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: External PR needs a contributor-owned real behavior proof update and maintainer choice on the legacy sidecar upgrade behavior before merge.

target.title: fix(codex): expose plugin tools in native conversation binding
target.body:
Fixes the native Codex conversation binding so plugin-registered OpenClaw tools are projected as Codex dynamic tools and dispatched back to plugin handlers.

What changed:
- builds the OpenClaw dynamic tool bridge for native bound Codex conversations
- passes plugin dynamic tool specs into thread/start
- refreshes stale/missing dynamic-tool bindings when plugin tools are available
- routes item/tool/call through the dynamic tool bridge instead of hardcoded failure
- adds regression coverage for projection and handler dispatch

Verification:
- corepack pnpm lint:extensions:bundled
- corepack pnpm test extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts
- corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
- corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
- corepack pnpm build
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Native bound Codex conversations could load external OpenClaw plugin tools in the runtime registry but still expose zero callable plugin tools to the Codex session. The real user evidence was Kynver AgentOS showing 25 registered agent_os_* runtime tools while Codex saw zero callable agent_os_* tools.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch fix/codex-plugin-dynamic-tools, Node v22.22.1, built OpenClaw CLI from this branch on Linux. The failing user environment is macOS, but the root issue is in the shared native Codex conversation binding code path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built the branch and ran the OpenClaw CLI from the produced checkout with `node openclaw.mjs --version`; also ran the native conversation-binding regression that exercises the app-server request path and dynamic tool call dispatch.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the patched branch:
```
$ node openclaw.mjs --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.17 (f66c95a)

$ corepack pnpm test extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)
```
- Observed result after fix: The patched native Codex binding now sends plugin dynamic tool specs during `thread/start` and dispatches `item/tool/call` through the OpenClaw dynamic tool bridge instead of returning the previous hardcoded "does not expose dynamic OpenClaw tools yet" failure.
- What was not tested: I did not run the patched OpenClaw build on the affected macOS user machine because I do not have access to that host.
target.author: totalsolutionsync
